skill.name: woocommerce-code-review
skill.description: Review WooCommerce code changes for coding standards compliance. Use when reviewing code locally, performing automated PR reviews, or checking code quality.
skill.summary: Review WooCommerce pull requests against backend, testing, and UI copy standards.
skill.author: woocommerce

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command execution, filesystem traversal, and weak cryptography patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown inline code examples, relative documentation links, and text matches, with no executable code or malicious instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: Static analysis interpreted Markdown inline code and fenced output examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines describe review rules and output format only, and they do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown documentation, not executable Ruby or shell code. No command interpreter, script block, or runtime invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated parent-directory Markdown links as path traversal. The links point to companion WooCommerce skill documentation and do not read files, write files, or accept user-controlled paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to static Markdown links such as references to other skill files. There is no file API use, path concatenation, or traversal of user-supplied input.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at the frontmatter description and a test naming rule. Manual review found no cryptographic function, hash selection, key handling, or encryption instruction on those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain descriptive text and a testing convention. No weak algorithm name is used in a cryptographic context.

content.value_statement: WooCommerce reviews often need project-specific standards beyond general PHP checks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through backend, test, and UI copy review rules.
actual_capabilities: Flags WooCommerce backend architecture issues such as standalone functions and incorrect dependency injection usage.
Checks PHP naming, docblock, validation, and WordPress coding convention expectations.
Reviews unit test naming, test documentation, and subject-under-test variable conventions.
Identifies UI text that should use sentence case under WooCommerce copy guidance.
Structures review feedback with issue type, location, standard, and suggested fix.
limitations: Depends on companion WooCommerce skills for full standards and examples.
Does not run tests, linters, or static analysis tools by itself.
Requires access to the changed files or diff to provide specific line comments.
Focuses on WooCommerce conventions, not broad application security auditing.

use_cases.title: Pre-submit WooCommerce review
use_cases.description: Review a local diff before opening a pull request and catch project convention issues early.

use_cases.title: Automated pull request screening
use_cases.description: Apply a consistent checklist for backend, testing, and UI copy issues in pull request comments.

use_cases.title: Standards coaching
use_cases.description: Explain why a WooCommerce code pattern violates a documented standard and suggest a compliant fix.

prompt_templates.title: Review a diff
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this WooCommerce diff for coding standards issues. Group findings by backend, tests, and UI copy. Include the file and line for each issue.

prompt_templates.title: Check backend architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these WooCommerce PHP changes for architecture, dependency injection, naming, docblocks, and validation problems. Prioritize violations that should block review.

prompt_templates.title: Audit test changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these WooCommerce test changes for naming, @testdox usage, subject-under-test naming, and file placement conventions. Suggest concise fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Perform full PR review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full WooCommerce standards review for this pull request. Cite the relevant standard for each finding and separate blocking issues from cleanup suggestions.

output_examples.input: A PHP service class is instantiated directly inside a class under src.
output_examples.output: Issue: Dependency injection violation. Location: the changed constructor or method. Standard: DI-managed classes should be requested from the container. Fix: inject or retrieve the service through the container pattern used by WooCommerce.

output_examples.input: A settings screen includes labels such as Save Changes and Payment Options.
output_examples.output: Issue: UI text uses title case. Location: the changed settings labels. Standard: WooCommerce UI copy should use sentence case. Fix: use Save changes and Payment options unless the term is a proper noun.

output_examples.input: A unit test names the tested object instance and omits a test description.
output_examples.output: Issue: Test convention mismatch. Location: the affected test method. Standard: use the subject-under-test naming convention and include test documentation. Fix: rename the variable and add a concise test description.

best_practices: Provide the exact diff or changed files so findings can include precise locations.
Ask for blocking issues first when reviewing large pull requests.
Keep companion WooCommerce standards available for complete context.
anti_patterns: Using the skill as a replacement for automated tests or linters.
Requesting generic PHP advice without WooCommerce context.
Submitting review comments without verifying the cited changed lines.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a review checklist and output format guide. It does not run commands or modify files.

faq.question: Can it review any WordPress plugin?
faq.answer: It can help with general patterns, but it is designed for WooCommerce standards.

faq.question: Does it require other skills?
faq.answer: It references companion WooCommerce skills for detailed standards and examples.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: A pull request diff, changed files, or focused code snippets with file paths work best.

faq.question: Can it review UI text?
faq.answer: Yes. It flags title case and checks text against WooCommerce sentence case guidance.

faq.question: Is it a security audit skill?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on WooCommerce coding standards and may not find security vulnerabilities.
